# User Story Template

## Purpose
Define user stories with clear context, acceptance criteria, and delivery requirements to support
backlog refinement and sprint planning.

## When to Use
- Use for individual backlog items that need detailed requirements and acceptance criteria.
- Use to align delivery teams on scope, testing, and dependencies for a single story.
- Use alongside (not instead of) the user story mapping template for release-level planning.

## Audience
Product Owner, Scrum Master, delivery team, QA, and stakeholders.

## Inputs
- Product vision and roadmap themes.
- Customer personas and journey insights.
- Definition of Ready/Done.
- Technical constraints and dependency maps.

## 1. Story Identification
- **ID:** (e.g., CRM-123)
- **Type:** Feature | Enhancement | Bug Fix | Technical Debt | Infrastructure
- **Epic Link:**
- **Sprint:**
- **Priority:** Critical | High | Medium | Low

## 2. User Story Description
**As a** [role/persona]

**I want** [capability/feature]

**So that** [benefit/value]

## 3. Detailed Description
Provide context, constraints, and business rules that help the team understand the story.

## 4. Acceptance Criteria
Use Given/When/Then to make criteria testable.

1. **Criterion 1**
   - **Given**
   - **When**
   - **Then**
2. **Criterion 2**
   - **Given**
   - **When**
   - **Then**
3. **Criterion 3**
   - **Given**
   - **When**
   - **Then**

## 5. Technical Considerations
- **Proposed Solution:**
- **API Dependencies:**
- **Database Changes:**
- **Security Considerations:**
- **Performance Expectations:**
- **Potential Impact Areas:**

## 6. UI/UX Specifications
- **Mockups/Wireframes:**
- **User Flow:**
- **Accessibility Requirements:**
- **Responsive Design Requirements:**

## 7. Definition of Done Checklist
- [ ] Acceptance criteria met
- [ ] Code reviewed and approved
- [ ] Unit tests written and passing
- [ ] Integration tests passing
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] UX/UI review completed
- [ ] Accessibility requirements met
- [ ] Performance benchmarks achieved
- [ ] Security review completed (if applicable)
- [ ] Product Owner approval obtained

## 8. Testing Notes
Document edge cases, test data, or risk-based testing considerations.

## 9. Estimation & Tracking
- **Story Points:**
- **Time Estimate (optional):**
- **Current Status:** To Do | In Progress | In Review | Testing | Done
- **Assignee:**
- **Reviewer:**

## 10. Related Items
- **Related Stories:**
- **Blocked By:**
- **Blocks:**
- **Related Documentation:**

## 11. Comments & Discussion
Capture open questions, decisions, and discussion notes.
